reasoning: The recent spike in the 5-year breakeven inflation rate (T5YIE) corresponds perfectly to the February CPI release on March 11, pushing the rate from 2.53 to 2.61 by March 13 (the latest value for March 15). At 2.61, the T5YIE is significantly above its recent trend (~2.40-2.50), and the statistical tool predicts a probability of 0.31 for the rate to increase further due to reversion to the mean. Between March 15 and March 22, the key event is the March 17-18 FOMC meeting. With recent presumably hot inflation, the Fed is expected to strike a hawkish tone regarding inflation expectations, which generally drives breakevens down (since higher real rates cool off long-run inflation). Given the high starting point of 2.61 and the likelihood of hawkish Fed policy signaling, the chances of the rate increasing further by March 22 remain relatively low and roughly align closely with the tool's 0.31 reversion probability estimate.
